english: to take place, to exist, čé-čuń-k̇yád žúgs-par mńón-pas (che chung khyad zhugs par mngon pas) as evidently a difference in size is existing (?) Dzl. 72, 3. II. pf. bčug (bcug) (perh. also žugs (zhugs) Lex.), fut. gžug (gzhug), imp. čug (cug), W. *čúg-če* (*cug ce*), vb. a., with náń-du (nang du) or termin.: 1. to put into, e.g. meat into a pan, a key into the key-hole, a culprit into prison; to infuse, inject, gžúg-par bẏa (gzhug par bya) this must be infused Med.; also fig. *nẏíń-rus čúg-če* (*nying rus cug ce*) W. to inspire with courage. In a special sense: a. dé-la blo ǰúg-pa (de la blo 'jug pa) to set one's mind on, to apply one's self to Glr. b. mi žig čós-la ǰúg-pa (mi zhig chos la 'jug pa) to convert a man, to induce him to adopt a certain religion; ǰúg-pa ('jug pa) also without an object, to missionate successfully Feer Introd. du B. au Cachem. 68. — 2. to make, render, appoint, constitute, with the accus. and termin., or col. with two accus.: mi žig rgýal-por ǰúg-pa (mi zhig rgyal por 'jug pa) to make one king Dzl.; mńón-du ǰúg-pa (mngon du 'jug pa) to make public, or manifest, to disclose, to show Samb.; *siń čúg-če* (*sing cug ce*) W. to clear, clarify; frq. with the supine or root of a verb: a. to cause, compel, prevail on, zar ǰúg-pa (zar 'jug pa) to prevail on another to eat something Dzl.; skród-du gžug-go (skrod du gzhug go) I shall induce (them) to expel (you) Dzl.; bžúgs ǰúg rgyu yin (bzhugs 'jug rgyu yin) he will induce (the god) to take his abode Glr.; gróńs-su ǰúg-pa (grongs su 'jug pa) to be the cause of somebody's death Mil.; yíd-la ǰúg-tu ǰúg-pa (yid la 'jug tu 'jug pa) to cause a thing to enter a person's mind, to put in mind, to remonstrate; ꜝpél-bar ǰúg-pa ('pel bar 'jug pa) (resp. mdzád-pa (mdzad pa)) = spél-ba (spel ba) to increase, as vb. a.; *ǰuń čúg-če* (*jung cug ce*) W. to cause to exist, create, procure; *kol ǰúg-če* (*kol 'jug ce*) W. = *skól-če* (*skol ce*) to cause to boil; dar-du čug čig (dar du cug cig) cause it to spread Glr. b. to command, order, bid, dmag ꜝdzín-du bčug (dmag 'dzin du bcug) he ordered the soldiers to take (the man) prisoner (but he escaped) Dzl. 222, 3; byéd-du ǰúg-pa (byed du 'jug pa) to bid one do a thing, frq.; btsún-mo blón-pos ꜝgebs-su bčug (btsun mo blon pos 'gebs su bcug) he gave orders for the queen being protected by the minister. c. to let, suffer, permit, smón-lam ꜝdébs-su čug (smon lam 'debs su cug) allow me to say a prayer; rtsíg-tu mi ǰúg (rtsig tu mi 'jug) I shall not give permission to build Glr. d. to give an opportunity Thgy. e. in a general sense: dal-du ǰúg-pa (dal du 'jug pa) to do things slowly, to be slow Mil. — 3. to put grammatically: sńón-du ǰúg-pa (sngon du 'jug pa) to put or place before, sńón-ǰug (sngon 'jug) a prefixed letter, a prefix; rǰés-ǰug (rjes 'jug) final letter, yáń-ǰug (yang 'jug) the last but one; also to put, to use a word in a certain signification, rgýu-mtsan-la ǰúg (rgyu mtshan la 'jug) is used with reference to cause Gram. — 4. to banish, to exile (prob. erron. for spẏúg-pa (spyug pa)), bẏáń-la (byang la) to northern regions Glr. — 5. sgo ǰúg-pa (sgo 'jug pa) v. sgo. — 6. inst. of ꜝbẏúg-pa ('byug pa).
sbst. 1. the going into, the entering; in a special sense 2. the beginning, the first stage of a disease Mng. — 3. (अवतार) the incarnation of a deity.
avarice, Dzl., Lex.; ǰúṅs-pa-čan ('jungs pa can) avaricious; ǰúṅs-ǰúr ('jungs jur) a miser, niggard.
, or ǰud-°tún-ma ('jud thun ma) Lex. ('accessible to all') a prostitute; ǰud-mtún byéd-pa ('jud mthun byed pa) to be a harlot.
, and more frq. ꜝdzúd-pa ('dzud pa), secondary forms of ǰúg-pa ('jug pa). Cf. čúd-pa (cud pa), °tsúd-pa (tsud pa).

, pf. bčún (bcun), fut. gžún (gzhun) (cf. bžún (bzhun), žun (zhun)) W. *čún-če* (*cun ce*), Cs.: to subdue, make tame; to make confess; W.; to make soft, to soften, e.g. iron; to punish, by words or blows; to convert.

, pf. bčúm (bcum), fut. gžum (gzhum), imp. čum (cum), to shudder, to shrink. (Acc. to grammatical analogy ǰúm-pa ('jum pa) ought to be vb. a., to cause to shudder, and °čúm-pa (cum pa) vb. n.) śa ǰúms-pa (sha 'jums pa) Lex., contraction of the muscles, shrinking, shuddering Sch.
, supine of ǰú-ba ('ju ba); ǰur mi °dód ('jur mi dod) indigestible Sch. (?).

1. (pf. bčur (bcur), q.v.) Cs.: complication; Sch. also: to struggle against, to resist. Pth.: ǰúr-bar gýúr-ba ('jur bar gyur ba) to be entangled; ǰúr-bu ('jur bu) Sch., *ǰúr-pa* (*'jur pa*) C. tangled yarn; srád-bui ǰúr (-pa) (srad bui 'jur pa) Lexx. w. e., Sch.: 'the tightness of the yarn'; ǰur-mtúg ('jur mthug) wrinkled, as the skin is in old age Thgy.; ǰur-míg ('jur mig) a wire-drawing plate, ǰur-míg-nas °drén-pa ('jur mig nas dren pa) to draw through this plate Thgy. — 2. = °dzúr-ba (dzur ba)
